{
  "claims": [
    "Governor Newsom's May Budget Revise includes a $12 billion plan to tackle homelessness, the largest investment of its kind in California history.",
    "The $12 billion investment will provide housing placements for 65,000 people and housing stability for more than 300,000 people.",
    "The May Revise budget aims to create 46,000 new housing units.",
    "The budget allocates $3.5 billion to address family homelessness with the goal of ending it within five years.",
    "A major expansion of the Golden State Stimulus will provide direct payments to two-thirds of Californians making up to $75,000.",
    "Qualified families with dependents, including undocumented families, will be eligible for an additional $500 under the expanded Golden State Stimulus."
